title: Design Systems
objectives: Adopt a shared design system in Flutter
Handle theming and light/dark modes cleanly

sections.markdown: A design system is the shared vocabulary of a product: **tokens** (colors, typography, spacing), **components** (buttons, inputs, cards), and **patterns** (empty states, error banners). It makes many screens look and behave like one product.

In Flutter, use Material 3 `ColorScheme.fromSeed` for a coherent palette and `ThemeExtension` for design-token additions Material doesn't cover. Wire dark mode through the same theme so light/dark differ by values, not by if/else in widgets.

Hard-coding colors, sizes and radii everywhere. Later you can't rebrand or theme; changes need find-and-replace. Read tokens/theme through context (`Theme.of(context)` and extensions), never literals.

flashcards.front: Three layers of a design system?
flashcards.back: Tokens (values), Components (widgets), Patterns (flows). Themes tie tokens into a coherent style.

flashcards.front: Why avoid raw colors in widgets?
flashcards.back: It kills theming, dark mode and rebranding — read through theme/tokens.
A way to add typed design tokens to Flutter's ThemeData beyond Material's defaults.

quiz.explain: Tokens are the primitives that themes and components consume.

quiz.q: Dark mode is best handled by…
options: Different theme values through the same widgets
quiz.explain: Themes swap values; widgets stay the same.

quiz.q: In Flutter, extending theme values beyond Material's defaults uses…
quiz.explain: ThemeExtension attaches typed extra tokens to ThemeData.

challenges.text: Extract spacing/radii/colors into an AppTokens class and use them.
Build AppButton/AppCard components that consume tokens/theme.
Add a ThemeExtension for semantic tokens (success/warn) and use in widgets.
Ship the design system as a separate package used by two apps with different brands.

interviewQuestions: What is a design system and its layers?
How do tokens differ from components?
How do you implement theming/dark mode in Flutter?
What is ThemeExtension and when use it?
How do you evolve a design system without breaking apps?
